title: How to Create Dynamic QR Codes in GoHighLevel — Drive Offline Conversions
description: Learn how to create dynamic QR codes in GoHighLevel to drive offline-to-online conversions. Direct users to payments, WhatsApp, funnels, forms, and more.
html_content:
QR codes have transformed from a novelty into a critical bridge between offline and online marketing. Whether you're running a restaurant, retail shop, or service-based business, QR codes let customers engage with your brand instantly—scanning a code at a physical location to access payment links, book appointments, join WhatsApp groups, or fill out forms.

The challenge? Most QR code generators are clunky, limited, and require juggling multiple tools. That's where GoHighLevel's QR Code Builder changes the game. In this guide, I'll show you exactly how to create dynamic, customizable QR codes that drive real offline-to-online conversions for your agency clients—and why this feature is now essential for any modern marketing stack.

If you're ready to see how this works firsthand, start your free 30-day GoHighLevel trial and test it with your own campaigns today.

📋 In This Guide

  • What Are Dynamic QR Codes and Why They Matter
  • How to Access the QR Code Builder in GoHighLevel
  • QR Code Redirect Options: Capture Every Lead Type
  • Step-by-Step: Creating Your First QR Code
  • Customization and Design Best Practices
  • Creating Bulk QR Codes for Contacts and Opportunities
  • Real-World Use Cases and Campaign Ideas

What Are Dynamic QR Codes and Why They Matter

Static QR codes are fixed—once printed, they cannot be changed. Dynamic QR codes, on the other hand, redirect to a URL that can be updated in real-time without changing the physical code itself. This means you can print thousands of codes and pivot your entire campaign strategy without reprinting anything.

For agencies managing multiple client campaigns, dynamic QR codes solve a massive problem: flexibility without waste. You can track scans, measure engagement, and adjust where the code points—all from your dashboard.

The real power emerges when you combine QR codes with GoHighLevel's ecosystem. Instead of directing users to a generic landing page, you can send them directly to:

  • Payment links for instant transactions
  • WhatsApp chats for immediate customer support
  • Sales funnels to nurture leads through your sales process
  • Forms and surveys to collect valuable data
  • Quizzes for engagement and lead qualification
  • Review pages to build social proof and credibility

This offline-to-online bridge is what drives conversions. A customer at your pop-up shop scans a QR code and lands directly in your payment funnel. A restaurant patron scans at their table and joins your loyalty program. An event attendee captures a code and books a consultation call.

How to Access the QR Code Builder in GoHighLevel

Accessing GoHighLevel's QR Code Builder is straightforward, but knowing where to find it is the first step.

Here's how:

  1. Log into your GoHighLevel account and navigate to the main dashboard
  2. Look for the "Integrations" or "Tools" section in your left sidebar
  3. Click on "QR Code Builder" (this may also appear under Marketing Tools or Campaign Tools depending on your version)
  4. You'll be taken to the QR Code management interface where you can create new codes, view existing ones, and manage redirects

If you can't locate it immediately, the feature may be available through your agency dashboard under a client's account settings. Make sure your GoHighLevel plan includes access to this feature—it's available on most Standard plans and above.

QR Code Redirect Options: Capture Every Lead Type

The flexibility of GoHighLevel's QR Code Builder lies in its redirect options. Here's what you can point a QR code to:

Payment Links — Direct customers to a secure payment page. Perfect for one-time purchases, event tickets, or digital product sales. No need for users to navigate your website; they scan and pay instantly.

WhatsApp Chats — Send users directly to a WhatsApp conversation with your team. Ideal for customer support, appointment booking, or sales inquiries. This eliminates friction and opens communication instantly.

Sales Funnels — Direct users into your pre-built funnels that guide them through your entire sales process. Whether it's a webinar funnel, VSL funnel, or multi-step sales sequence, QR codes serve as the offline entry point.

Forms — Capture leads with custom forms built in GoHighLevel. Attendees at events, customers in-store, or prospects at trade shows can submit their information with a single scan.

Surveys and Quizzes — Gather feedback or qualify leads through interactive surveys and quizzes. This works exceptionally well for post-purchase follow-ups or product research.

Review Pages — Boost your online reputation by directing customers to leave reviews. Google reviews, testimonials, and case studies all become more accessible when there's no friction.

Each redirect option is tracked, so you'll see exactly how many scans your QR code receives and where users are being directed.

Step-by-Step: Creating Your First QR Code

Let me walk you through the actual creation process so you can implement this today.

Step 1: Click "Create New QR Code"

From the QR Code Builder dashboard, click the button to create a new QR code. You'll be taken to the creation interface.

Step 2: Choose Your Redirect Type

Select which destination you want the QR code to point to. The dropdown will show all available options: Payment Link, WhatsApp, Funnel, Form, Survey, Quiz, Review Page, or Custom URL.

Step 3: Select the Specific Asset

If you're redirecting to a funnel, select which funnel. If it's a form, choose which form. GoHighLevel will populate all your existing assets automatically, making selection seamless.

Step 4: Generate the QR Code

Click the generate button. GoHighLevel creates a unique dynamic QR code that's ready to use immediately.

Step 5: Download and Deploy

Download the QR code as a PNG or PDF file. You can now print it, add it to your marketing materials, post it on social media, or embed it in digital campaigns.

💡 Pro Tip

Name your QR codes descriptively (e.g., "Event-2024-Payment-Link" or "Store-Location-WhatsApp") so you can quickly identify which code is which when you have multiple active campaigns.

Customization and Design Best Practices

A generic black-and-white QR code blends into the background. To maximize scans, customize your codes to match your brand.

Add Your Logo — Most QR code builders, including GoHighLevel's, allow you to embed your logo or brand icon in the center. This increases brand recognition and scans.

Use Brand Colors — Customize the foreground and background colors to match your brand palette. Just ensure enough contrast so the code remains scannable.

Test Before Printing — Always test your QR code with multiple devices (iPhone, Android) before committing to large print runs. A quick scan confirms the redirect works.

Size Matters — Ensure your QR code is large enough. Minimum recommended size is 2cm x 2cm for reliable scanning. Larger is better, especially in print materials.

Placement Strategy — Position your QR code where users naturally pause. On a menu? At eye level. On a poster? Prominent placement near the main message. In an email? Near the call-to-action.

Creating Bulk QR Codes for Contacts and Opportunities

For agencies managing large contact lists or multiple opportunities, creating codes one-by-one is inefficient. GoHighLevel supports bulk QR code generation.

How It Works:

You can generate unique QR codes for each contact in your database or for different opportunities in your pipeline. Each code can direct to a personalized funnel, form, or payment link specific to that contact or deal.

This is particularly powerful for:

  • Multi-location businesses — Create unique codes for each location with location-specific WhatsApp numbers or forms
  • Affiliate networks — Generate unique codes for each affiliate partner that tracks their referrals
  • Event check-ins — Create personalized codes for VIP attendees or different ticket tiers
  • Direct mail campaigns — Print individualized codes that personalize the follow-up experience

The automation here saves hours of manual work while providing better data tracking and personalization.

Real-World Use Cases and Campaign Ideas

Restaurant and Hospitality: Place QR codes on tables linking to a digital menu, payment system, and review request. Customers scan, order, pay, and review—all in seconds.

Retail Pop-Up Shops: Use QR codes to direct customers to a limited-time offer funnel or exclusive product launch. Track how many people engaged with your physical location.

Real Estate: Print QR codes on property signs that direct to virtual tours, pricing information, or appointment booking forms. Potential buyers get instant access without leaving the neighborhood.

Event Marketing: QR codes on event posters, flyers, and sponsorship materials drive registration. Post-event, use codes on thank-you cards to direct attendees to feedback surveys.

Service Providers: Add QR codes to invoices, receipts, and direct mail pieces directing customers to book their next appointment or leave a review.

B2B Networking: QR codes on business cards direct prospects directly to your calendar booking page or a brief qualification quiz, eliminating back-and-forth emails.

The key insight: QR codes are friction-removal tools. They eliminate the gap between physical and digital, between awareness and action.

GoHighLevel's QR Code Builder makes this effortless for you and your agency clients. You're not just creating codes—you're building entire offline-to-online conversion systems.
